# fix(dataflow): validate workflow.name before filename interpolation

<!-- Generated by SessionEnd hook from a commit matching a journal-worthy pattern.
     Next session: review, then promote to a real journal entry OR delete. -->

**Commit**: `a17fa57ded2a` — fix(dataflow): validate workflow.name before filename interpolation

**Body**:

The CLI `dataflow generate docs` subcommand interpolated `workflow.name`
directly into a filesystem path via `f"{workflow.name}.md"`, so any
non-string, path-traversal substring, or filesystem-unsafe character
was written to disk verbatim. The originating leak was a Mock-object
repr (`<Mock name='test_workflow.name' id='...'>`) escaping the
`tests/unit/cli/test_generate_command.py` Tier-1 boundary because
`Mock(name="X")` sets the Mock's repr-name and NOT the `.name`
attribute — `.name` returns a child Mock that f-strings to the repr.
108 orphan files accumulated in `docs/` (107) and
`packages/kailash-dataflow/docs/` (1) since 2026-04-15.

Two-layered fix per autonomize directive ("root cause over symptom"):

  1. Production code now validates at the trust boundary regardless
     of test-side correctness. New `dataflow.utils.filenames` exports
     `safe_workflow_filename(name, ext)` and `WorkflowNameError` (a
     `ValueError` subclass). Strict allowlist
     `^[A-Za-z0-9_][A-Za-z0-9_.-]{0,127}$`; rejects path-traversal
     substring `..`; logs at WARN with hashed sha256[:8] fingerprint
     per observability.md Rule 8 (raw input never echoed); error
     messages carry actionable Mock-anti-pattern hints.

  2. Test correctly constructs the Mock (post-construction
     `.name = "X"` via `_make_workflow_mock()` helper) and uses
     `tmp_path` for `--output-dir` per `tests/unit/CLAUDE.md` Tier-1
     filesystem-isolation contract — `Path.write_text` is NOT caught
     by `patch("builtins.open", mock_open())`.

Plus a regression test that exercises the historical Mock-leak vector
end-to-end through the Click runner, asserting `exit_code == 2` and
zero `<Mock` files written. Same-shard fix-immediately disposition
per autonomous-execution.md Rule 4 (security-reviewer surface
extension).

Includes:
  - 52 Tier-1 regression tests for `safe_workflow_filename` (9 accept,
    23 reject including path-traversal / control chars / shell
    metacharacters / Unicode bidi / Mock-repr / non-string, 9 ext
    validation, error/log hygiene).
  - 1 Tier-1 regression test in test_generate_command.py.
  - kailash-dataflow 2.7.7 → 2.7.8 (patch; pyproject + __version__ +
    CHANGELOG bumped atomically per zero-tolerance Rule 5 +
    build-repo-release-discipline Rule 5).
  - Workspace journal 0005 §F4 disposition recorded.

Co-Authored-By: Claude Opus 4.7 (1M context) <noreply@anthropic.com>
